context: Checkpoint 2026-04-29 preparing for compaction after additional atomic streaming slices. Main lane remains host-proxied LLM streaming with reusable non-LLM primitives. User explicitly corrected architecture: anything not LLM-specific should be generalized; WASM is useful but not an architectural prison. We kept generic SSE framing/reader in tractor::streaming and LLM semantics in the bridge layer.
current_tasks: Latest unpushed commits on top of origin/develop include fd40b0cc docs(agent): describe synthesized stream final json; b15d7e7f docs(agent): note synthesized stream final body; 95a07d0f feat(tractor): synthesize stream final response; 6f9266d7 test(tractor): cover host stream bridge with sse server; fddc8873 chore(project): refresh stream reader handoff; e87e0218 docs(agent): note stream bridge reader seam; 49231f06 feat(tractor): read stream bridge through sse seam; 53638588 feat(tractor): add incremental llm stream reader seam; 803a97d3 feat(tractor): add generic sse reader primitive; plus prior stream stack back to 2b91ffe1.

Generic stream primitive: packages/tractor/src/streaming/sse_events.rs has SseDataEventBuffer, parse_sse_data_events, and read_sse_data_events_limited(reader,max_len,on_event). This is provider-neutral/target-neutral and tested for partial frames, CRLF, body limits, callback errors, and body return.

LLM stream layer: packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge/llm_stream_events.rs consumes generic SSE events, parses OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic stream deltas, persists partial AgentResponse chunks to NativeSync, tracks last_sequence/stored_chunks, and synthesizes parser-compatible final JSON if chunks were stored.

Final JSON synthesis: OpenAI-compatible provider families get choices[0].message.content plus zero usage; anthropic gets content:[{type:'text',text}] plus zero usage. This keeps existing pi-agent final parsers viable once the stream bridge is used.

complete-http-stream path: packages/tractor/src/host/wasi_bridge/core.rs now uses send_llm_http_post for host-enforced route/body/header/auth, then reads resp.into_reader() through store_stream_agent_response_chunks_from_reader with MAX_LLM_RESPONSE_BODY_LEN. This means complete SSE frames can be persisted as response bytes are read.

New test coverage: complete_http_stream_persists_sse_chunks_from_host_response uses a local fake HTTP SSE server and verifies stored partial AgentResponse rows and synthesized final JSON; complete_http_stream_preserves_route_enforcement verifies host route enforcement before accepting mismatched base_url.
ROADMAP and ADR-053 updated to document generic stream primitives, SSE reader seam, and synthesized final JSON strategy.
blockers: streaming_reader_available() still false. We have host streaming internals and final JSON synthesis, but no full pi-agent/harness proof that enabling provider stream:true produces correct final AgentResponse + visible partials end-to-end.
No GitHub Actions run yet for the large local stack. Current stack is long; push only after deciding this batch is ready and then watch with gh run watch --exit-status.

The local pi-lens monitor/LSP pipeline repeatedly crashed on some edits with memory access out of bounds; code checks still passed. Mention if diagnosing tooling, but it did not affect Rust validation.
Full tractor-ts unit suite remains noisy locally due missing WASM fixtures; avoid broad TS suite unless working on fixtures.
Disk after clean:light: ~12GB free; Rust targets ~4.86GB.
next_actions: Next slice after compaction: add a pi-agent/Tractor harness-style test or smaller integration proving complete-http-stream + synthesized final JSON works through the plugin-side http_post_stream_via_host seam. Avoid full broad suites.

Then consider flipping streaming_reader_available() only behind tests proving: provider request body includes stream:true, route enforcement/credentials remain host-owned, partial AgentResponse chunks are stored/observable, final AgentResponse sequence follows last partial, and final content is correct.
Before push: git status --short; git log --oneline origin/develop..HEAD; run scoped validations below; then git push origin develop and gh run watch --exit-status for the CI run.

Scoped validations already run after latest changes: cd packages/pi-agent && cargo check --target wasm32-wasip1 --quiet; cd packages/tractor && cargo check --quiet; cargo test --lib complete_http_stream --quiet; cargo test --lib store_stream_agent_response_chunks_from_reader --quiet; cargo test --lib synthesize_stream_final_response_body --quiet; cargo test --lib read_sse_data_events_limited --quiet; npm run project:validate --silent; git diff --check; npm run clean:light --silent.
Potential next design cleanup: extract duplicated OpenAI/Anthropic SSE delta parsing into shared target-neutral crate/module used by pi-agent and Tractor, but only if value outweighs churn.
open_questions: Is synthesized final JSON the committed long-term contract, or should WIT stream-response-result eventually carry a stream summary/content separately from final_body?
Should host partials remain direct AgentResponse nodes or evolve to generic StreamChunk observations with projection to AgentResponse for clients?
When exactly should streaming_reader_available() flip true: after host fake server test plus pi-agent wasm check, or only after full pi-agent harness proves WebSocket/client observability?
How should stream errors/cancellations be represented if partial chunks were already stored?
Should read_sse_data_events_limited become public API beyond crate-private once other host/plugin features need it?
key_decisions_pending: End-to-end readiness criteria and test shape for enabling provider stream:true.
Long-term final response contract for streaming providers.
AgentResponse direct chunks vs generic StreamChunk/dual-write.
Shared stream parser extraction vs current duplicated LLM parser logic.
When to push the large unpushed stack.
